Home / Time Tracking / Alternatives to Harvest

2 open source alternatives to Harvest

Time tracking with invoicing built in. Here are the open source projects real teams use instead — ranked by fit, with honest pros and cons for each.

What people don't love about Harvest

  • Project structure is rigid for complex agencies.
  • Integrations feel dated compared to newer entrants.
  • Invoicing is basic — many teams pair it with a second tool.

Current Harvest pricing (for reference): Free for 1 user; Pro $10.80/user/month.

Ad slot — above comparison

Quick comparison

Alternative Best for License Self-host Hosted cloud?
Kimai
Self-hosted time tracker with invoicing and reports. 		The closest self-hosted match for Harvest's timesheet-plus-invoicing workflow. AGPL-3.0 ★★☆☆☆ Yes
InvoicePlane
Simple invoicing for freelancers and consultants. 		Pairing simple time tracking with solid invoicing for contractors. MIT ★☆☆☆☆ Self-host only

1. Kimai — The closest self-hosted match for Harvest's timesheet-plus-invoicing workflow.

Self-hosted time tracker with invoicing and reports.

Strengths

  • Teams, projects, customers and invoices in one tool.
  • REST API plus mobile clients.
  • Easy PHP/MySQL deployment on any VPS.

Weaknesses

  • UI is functional rather than polished.
  • Some reports need the paid plugins or plugin-store extensions.
  • Smaller ecosystem of integrations than Toggl.
License: AGPL-3.0 Self-host difficulty: 2/5 Hosted cloud optionMobile: iOS, Android

Kimai homepage · Source on GitHub · Harvest vs Kimai →

2. InvoicePlane — Pairing simple time tracking with solid invoicing for contractors.

Simple invoicing for freelancers and consultants.

Strengths

  • Runs on cheap PHP/MySQL hosting.
  • Focused on the invoicing workflow.
  • Great for solo freelancers.

Weaknesses

  • Development pace has slowed in recent years.
  • Feature set narrower than Akaunting.
  • UI is functional, not modern.
License: MIT Self-host difficulty: 1/5

InvoicePlane homepage · Source on GitHub · Harvest vs InvoicePlane →
